signals.headline: Goldman Sachs Releases U
signals.raw:
Goldman Sachs Releases U.S. Inflation Monitor for March 2026 (April 20)** | YES | HIGH  
Goldman Sachs published its inflation playbook on April 20, 2026, confirming March headline CPI surged 0.87% monthly to 3.29% YoY and PCE to 3.45% YoY, driven by a 10.9% energy spike from the Iran war (oil from $71 to $103/barrel), while core inflation decelerates toward 2.2% (excluding tariffs) with forecasts
matched_markets: Will annual inflation increase by 3.8% in April?

signals.headline: No newer confirmations post-April 20** | NO | HIGH
signals.raw:
No newer confirmations post-April 20** | NO | HIGH  
March CPI data (released ~April 10) showed 0.9% monthly/3.3% YoY headline rise, core at 0.2% monthly/2.6% YoY, with 75% of gains from gasoline (up 21.2%) tied to Iran war; no April data or resolutions reported today.[2][1]
